contracts.approach: Implemented the v3 Phase-0 contract layer exactly per DESIGN-V3.md after reading the full v3 spec, the v1/v2 docs, and every file touched plus their consumers (hud.js/screens.js/input.js/spawner.js/objects.js/catalog.js/absorb.js) to map boot-time couplings. tuning.js gained the full v3 constant set (GOAL_*/SKYTREE_*, shop/terrain, map edge, curated/density, feedback, donack, rank S240/A330/B450/C600, DRAW_CALL_CAP 72 with the 64-worst ledger, LS v3 keys) with a complete @deprecated MOON_* alias block; events.js gained EVT.GOAL_CALL/GOAL_GUIDE/GOAL_CONTACT (new wire strings 'goalCall' etc.) with MOON_* aliases sharing the same strings, EVT.LANDMARK/COLLECT, extended/aliased PAYLOADS, and the frozen v3 ABSORB subscription-order + DUAL-TAG header contract. types.js got all v3 typedef additions (AbsorbEvent.archetypeCode/collectibleId, ScoreEvent.archetypeCode, GoalEvent.collectFound, Goal*/Landmark/Collect events with deprecated Moon* typedef aliases, CuratedPlacement/LandmarkDef/CollectibleDef/ZoneRect/TerrainWall/TerrainPrism). tiers.js was rewritten to the 7-tier Tokyo table with 70 frozen snake_case ids (v2 id strings reused where the design's recipe-reuse mapping applies), 7-entry sky arrays, asserts 7/70, non-decreasing moonAngSize, and the extended floored fog/load pair assert. index.html got the full mobile-first CSS rewrite (top bar, @property --gauge dash ring + @supports bar fallback, safe-area vars, landscape + 768px breakpoints, band matrix) plus all new DOM (#top-bar, #absorbed-inline, #donack-root as direct body child, #collect-popup, #collection-grid/#result-collect-n, #donack-toggle, #goal-arrow, 8 donack webp preloads, .dk-* classes) while keeping v2-consumer ids alive via three clearly-marked transitional shims. main.js became the v3 skeleton: V3-WIRE no-op stubs for CityTerrain/CuratedSpawner/Collection/Donack with frozen interface shapes, terrain injected into BallPhysics, curated.update call site, v3 reset ownership, devTeleport(name) + ?at= boot path, updated binding frame-order header. scaleManager.js (GOAL_RADIUS_M read) and ballPhysics.js (optional terrain param, collide after XZ integration) carry the two documented exceptions. DESIGN-V3.md gained the Phase-0 appendix (frozen id tables, exemptions ledger, reset-ownership table, alias retirement plan, shim deletion list). Exit criterion verified live: npm run build passes, and via vite preview + agent-browser the game boots, starts, absorbs (score 331, 2.0cm->2.5cm growth), and renders the new HUD correctly at desktop and 390px portrait with zero page errors and zero stream files touched.
keyDecisions: Froze the 70 English snake_case chunk archetype ids (the design table only gave Japanese names): full English<->Japanese table in the DESIGN-V3.md Phase-0 appendix; v2 id strings reused where the design's recipe-reuse mapping applies (bicycle/person/car/skyscraper/mountain/torii plus eraser, paperclip, cat, trash_can, utility_pole, bus, truck, kiosk, crane, gas_tank, stadium, cruise_ship, ferris_wheel) so the transitional build keeps substantial visible content.

Froze the EXTRA code map: collectible archetypeCode = 70 + COLLECTIBLE_ID (80 = ハチ公像, dual-tagged landmarkId 0); 82..91 = the 10 non-Hachiko landmark archetypes; 92 = shop shell; 93 = Skytree display slot. Froze landmarkId enum 0..10 in threshold-ladder order.

EVT alias mechanics: GOAL_* get the NEW wire strings ('goalCall'/'goalGuide'/'goalContact') and MOON_* aliases repeat the same strings inside the frozen EVT literal; PAYLOADS.moonCall/moonGuide/moonContact are the same object identities as the goal* entries — every v2 emitter/subscriber pair stays consistent unmodified.
MOON_DIR_MIN_ELEV is deliberately NOT deprecated (the sky-dome moon stays as a night cosmetic) — it is the sole permanent exemption to the integrator's `grep -rn 'MOON_' src == 0` gate.

Wired the v3 constructor signatures NOW with harmless extra args ignored by the v2 implementations: new BallPhysics(bus, terrain), new Absorb(bus, scaleMgr, CATALOG, curated), new RunStats(bus, scaleMgr, worldSeed, collection), new CameraRig(camera, bus, {clampBoom, interior01}) — streams implement against them without main.js churn.
Desktop top-bar dissolution uses display:contents at >=768px so one DOM serves both layouts; backdrop-filter only exists inside the >=768px @supports block (Android compositor saving).

Collection grid gated CSS-only: #win-overlay.staged #collection-grid-wrap{opacity:0} means the v2 screens.js never reveals it (clean transitional behavior); Stream D only adds the 1.9s .result-reveal cue.

tiers.js extended fog/load assert implemented as: max(FOG_FAR_K, FOG_FAR_MIN_M/ws_t) < max(loadRadiusSim, LOAD_RADIUS_MIN_M/ws_t) - cellSizeSim at each tier's native worldScale ws_t = 0.04*5^t (T0 floors bind: 225 < 249.25).
deviations: index.html keeps three TRANSITIONAL shims the spec's final state deletes, because the untouched v2 hud.js dereferences them at boot: (1) #dash-gauge/#dash-gauge-fill kept as a small visible bar above the dash button (v3 ring CSS also shipped), (2) #moon-arrow kept as a functioning alias element alongside the new #goal-arrow, (3) #absorbed-value kept NESTED inside #absorbed-inline ('x0'). All listed with deletion owners in the DESIGN-V3.md Phase-0 appendix.

Dash-ring fallback: the spec's 'same --gauge variable consumed as width %' is not expressible in CSS (angle->percent division). Frozen instead: hud.js writes BOTH --gauge (0-360deg, ring) and --gauge01 (0-1, fallback bar via scaleX + transform transition). DASH_READY flash class target is #dash-button.flash.

npm run dev does NOT boot until Stream C lands: catalog.js and objects.js (stream-owned files) carry DEV asserts hard-coded to 60 ids that now conflict with the 70-id tiers.js. Prod build strips them and runtime is null-tolerant (unknown archetypes render as empty geometry), so the binding exit criterion (npm run build + boot) was verified via vite preview. Documented prominently in the appendix.
MAP_BOUNDS single source of truth placed in tuning.js (the spec lists it both under tuning and as a cityMap.js export); cityMap.js must re-export the tuning object verbatim.

devTeleport uses a local frozen DEV_STARTS table in main.js (cityMap.js does not exist yet); V3-WIRE comment tells the integrator to switch to the cityMap import. spawner.onTeleport() is called guarded (typeof check) since Stream B adds it.

EXTRA code 93 assigned to a 東京スカイツリー display-name-only slot (never store-spawned) to make DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE length exactly 94 — the spec's '11 landmarks + 12 collectibles + shop shell = 24' double-counts the dual-tagged ハチ公像; this resolution is frozen in the appendix.
The v2 finale now triggers at the v3 values through the aliases (called 380m, contact 420m instead of 420/500) — intended consequence of the alias protocol; the run is still completable.

streams.approach: Read DESIGN-V3.md in full plus the v2 spec and every Phase-0 contract file (types/events/tuning/tiers, main.js V3-WIRE markers, scaleManager pattern), then implemented Stream A against the frozen interfaces. Created render/goalTower.js: SkytreeView with a 634-tri merged vertex-colored lattice (4 tapered leg segments, brace rings, core, 2 decks at 350m/450m, antenna; fog:false Basic) plus one additive glow/beam mesh (setGlow01/setBeamPulse) — exactly 2 draws; sim pose derived live from SKYTREE_POS/worldScale minus a self-tracked rebase shift (RESCALE/REBASE/GAME_RESET subscriptions); mesh<->silhouette handoff latch (ws >= SKY_SILHOUETTE_WS_MAX && simDist < 0.8*CAMERA_FAR, 10% release hysteresis, 2s opacity crossfade). Rewrote game/finale.js: idle->called(380m, GOAL_CALL + beam pulse)->approach(gameplay live, 10Hz GOAL_GUIDE at 0.55*towerHeight, XZ contact test vs ballR + towerR*GOAL_CONTACT_PAD)->contact->merge(ball lerp into the tower axis, hide at 0.6s)->ascension(anchor rises 40r, beginNightFade, ascensionBurst)->afterglow->done; descent/landing/magnet math deleted; _simCache {towerX,towerZ,towerR,mergeFrom*,ascendBaseY} with own RESCALE/REBASE handlers plus per-frame pre-contact refresh from SkytreeView; skipCinematic and the stale-camera-matrix guide skip kept; finale also drives skytree.update + env.setGoalSilFade every frame. cameraRig.js gained the frozen 3rd constructor arg {clampBoom, interior01}: interior dist/height multipliers crossfaded by a damped interior01 (snaps with the springs), boom clamp applied to the position TARGET before the spring. environment.js: palettes now TIERS.length+NIGHT (7+1), FOG_FAR_MIN_M floor applied at query time via a local worldScale tracker (boot 0.04, exact 10Hz resync from GROW, /= S in rescale()), Tokyo Bay water quad pair + L-shaped quay strip (+2 draws, real-meter group rescaled/rebased exactly), and the new uGoalSil* sky-shader silhouette whose azimuth/angular size are recomputed per frame from camera->SKYTREE_POS (angle-matched to the mesh by construction); night moon disc kept untouched. effects.js: EVT.LANDMARK gold ring burst, setCollectibleProvider (shared twinkle callback with the rare provider), moonBurst renamed ascensionBurst, dustRing deleted. render/moon.js deleted last. Headless-tested everything via a /tmp suite (50 assertions, all green): tri budget, pose/rescale/rebase/reset math, handoff crossfade timing, fog floor, silhouette uniform angle-match, bay transforms, full finale ramp (8.7s at 1x, 1.8s skipped), event once-semantics, latch ordering.
keyDecisions: SkytreeView derives its sim pose from SKYTREE_POS / scaleMgr.worldScale (read LIVE) minus a self-tracked rebase shift, instead of caching simX/simZ — mathematically identical under RESCALE (*=S) / REBASE (-=sx,sz) but additionally correct after devTeleport's direct worldScale snap at boot (?at=goal&r=400).

uGoalSil* are NEW uniforms added alongside the KEPT uMoon* night-cosmetic uniforms — the spec says both 'rename uMoon*->uGoalSil*' and 'night moon disc kept'; a literal rename would have destroyed the kept moon, so the slot was duplicated (machinery reuse) rather than renamed.

env silhouette visibility is gated by uGoalSilFade (default 1, finale-driven from SkytreeView.silFade01), NOT by a ws<0.2 check in env — closes the spec's visibility gap (ws in [0.2,~1] where simDist > 0.8*CAMERA_FAR would otherwise show NEITHER representation, breaking the navigation-anchor-from-frame-one promise); the ws gate lives on the MESH side (takeover requires ws >= SKY_SILHOUETTE_WS_MAX).
Handoff is reversible with 10% distance hysteresis (v2 was one-way descent; the v3 camera roams), with a 2s opacity crossfade on the mesh mirroring the v2 sky-fade pacing.

environment tracks worldScale locally (boot 0.04, exact resync from every 10Hz GROW payload trueRadius/simRadius, /=S in rescale()) because its constructor signature (scene, camera) is frozen and main.js is untouchable — used only for cosmetics (fog floor, bay scale, silhouette size), all self-correcting within 0.1s of play.

Finale merge target = (towerX, mergeFromY, towerZ): the ball slides horizontally into the glowing tower axis; ascension rises the hidden anchor (ball.pos parked on it) so the effects fountain + camera pullback ride it — v2 machinery verbatim with the monument static.
0.8*CAMERA_FAR mirrored as a local const (3200) in goalTower.js since renderer.js does not export CAMERA_FAR.
effects.dustRing deleted (v3 has no landing state; finale was the sole consumer, both files Stream A).
deviations: Bay water surface at y = +0.3m real instead of the spec's -0.3m: the v1 ground plane is an opaque y=0 sheet covering the whole visible area, so a submerged quad would never render; the quay wall (3m) hides the shoreline seam. Documented in the environment.js header.

Spec line 'silhouette active worldScale<0.2' implemented as: silhouette active while uGoalSilFade>0 (finale-driven), mesh takeover gated by ws >= SKY_SILHOUETTE_WS_MAX && simDist < 0.8*CAMERA_FAR — strictly more correct (no representation gap) while honoring both spec clauses; the silhouette's angular size is geometrically true at any ws so it degrades gracefully.
uMoon* uniforms NOT renamed (kept night cosmetic per the same spec section); uGoalSil* added as a parallel slot.
v2 LANDED dust ring + soft magnet removed entirely (MOON_MAGNET_*/MOON_LAND_* now have zero consumers — they are tuning.js alias-block residents the integrator deletes).
SkytreeView additionally self-subscribes GAME_RESET (zero shift, drop handoff latch) — additive to the frozen interface; not in the reset-ownership table but self-contained like env/backdrop.

streams.approach: Implemented Stream B (world data + spawn) against the frozen Phase-0 contracts. cityMap.js authors the full hakoniwa in real meters with ORIGIN = BALL START: SHOP (walls W1-W5, prisms P1-P4 per spec coordinates), 411 PLACEMENTS expanded at module load from cluster records via mulberry32(0x544f4b59) (236 interior incl. the P4-table/shelf-mid items, 71 street, 22 gutter, 55 district incl. the scramble crowd x16, 12 collectibles, 12 landmark placements incl. 3 bridge spans, 1 release-gated shop shell), ZONES + bandAllowedAt() (first-match rects, T6 map-wide, bands 0/1 nowhere, mask 0 in-shop/out-of-bounds), LANDMARKS (frozen ids 0..10 w/ EXTRA codes 82..91), COLLECTIBLE_IDS explicit enum 0..11 (codes 70+id), SKYTREE_POS, MAP_BOUNDS re-export from tuning, DEV_STARTS, and validateCityMap() implementing every spec assert (aisle table, ball-start/exit-lane clearance, per-placement 3D-reach via 1+PICKUP_FORGIVE_K with prism inset, NO-SEAL cbrt < 0.5*gate, growth budget full-clear 0.539m, growth-chain table 21/30/34/35 ≥ 8, ladder print, SKYTREE_COLLIDER_K < GOAL_CONTACT_PAD, collectible-id uniqueness, singleton placement counts). terrain.js implements CityTerrain: per-substep circle-vs-AABB shop collision (pre-release), permanent Skytree base circle w/ BOUNCE + 0.25s cooldown (skipped after EVT.GOAL_CONTACT so the finale MERGE is never fought), bounds hard clamp + 4r soft edge damping, interiorAt01 (smooth 1.5m falloff, 0 post-fade), 3D slab-method clampCameraBoom (deactivates only AFTER the 0.6s fade), flightWallTest, a hand-built merged vertex-colored mesh (+1 draw, real-meter geometry riding scale=1/worldScale + origin position), worldScale read live from scaleMgr and origin via RESCALE/REBASE subscriptions. curated.js implements CuratedSpawner: SoA flatten of PLACEMENTS, FLAG_CURATED|FLAG_RARE allocation from the shared store, dynamic re-banding (synchronous re-stamp in the TIER_UP handler so the immediately-following hash rebuild ingests it, plus per-visit re-stamp + DEV window assert), activation vs max(loadRadiusSim, LOAD_RADIUS_MIN_M/ws)+objR with the sub-pixel gate skipped for collectibles/landmarks both ways ('never lost' without 'always alive'), consume=false ring/sub-pixel deactivation with the own consumed bitmask set only on absorb, deferred ABSORB bookkeeping (handler sets the consumed bit + queues; never reads instanceSlot; EXTRA pool slots freed from curated's own remembered slot — exclusive ownership since POOL_BY_CODE can't see codes ≥ 70), EVT.LANDMARK queued and emitted at the next update (step 3, same render frame, strictly after the ABSORB chain ⇒ COLLECT-before-LANDMARK by construction), terrain-release mirror (shell unlock + 0.6s elevated y-drop), collectibleIdFor/forEachAliveCollectible/forceScan(deferred full pass + origin re-anchor)/reset, RESCALE/REBASE origin tracking, 300-update ownership identity DEV assert. spawner.js gains the zone-mask skip right after the deterministic-draw marker (native→real bridge = native * WS0 * 5^band), DENSITY_K_V3 wantK scaling, the LOAD_RADIUS_MIN_M ring floor in band-native units, floored fog in the prewarm skip + fadeIn check, FLAG_CURATED skips in _onAbsorb/_subPixelSweep + DEV assert in _despawnIndex, reinject DEV assert archIdx>=0, POW5 headroom to 5^12, an optional 7th scaleMgr param, and onTeleport() (unload-all, queue clear, origin 0, scale-exponent resync, consumed bitmasks kept). Verified headless under node: validateCityMap green, 30 assertions across terrain/curated/spawner/ownership test scripts (in /tmp, not committed) all pass, including same-seed determinism with masks, forced-TIER_UP re-banding into [t-1,t+1], deferred absorb + consumed identity, ring-deactivate→reactivate, the dual-tag LANDMARK frame ordering, shop release/shell activation, skytree bounce, and chunk+curated === store alive identity across mixed absorbs.
keyDecisions: FLAG_CURATED declared as a local const 16 in both spawner.js and curated.js (value frozen by Phase 0) instead of importing from objects.js, which does not export it until Stream C lands — avoids a build break in the B-before-C integration order; integrator may unify the import sites afterwards.

Dynamic re-banding is SYNCHRONOUS inside the EVT.TIER_UP handler (tight loop over alive curated slots, typically tens of byte writes) rather than amortized: ScaleManager rebuilds the banded hashes immediately after the TIER_UP emit returns, so stamping first is the only way 're-banded slots enter the live hashes for free' actually holds. The 64/frame round-robin still re-stamps on visit and DEV-asserts the window.

EVT.LANDMARK is queued in the ABSORB handler and emitted at the start of the next curated.update() — same render frame (absorb = step 2, curated.update = step 3) and strictly after the full ABSORB dispatch, which makes COLLECT-before-LANDMARK hold by construction for the dual-tagged ハチ公.

EXTRA pool slot lifecycle: the main attach handler can only free chunk pools (POOL_BY_CODE covers codes 0..69), so curated frees EXTRA slots (codes >= 70) from its own remembered slot in the deferred tick — satisfies the slot-steal convention (the ABSORB handler never reads store.instanceSlot) without double-free risk.

Sub-pixel gating is skipped for collectibles/landmarks on ACTIVATION as well as despawn — otherwise a 30cm collectible at a T4/T5 district (e.g. 特上大トロ at Tokyo Station) could never activate and 'never lost' would be violated.

terrain.js and curated.js read scaleMgr.worldScale LIVE each call (origin shift stays event-driven via REBASE) — this makes them automatically correct across devTeleport's direct worldScale writes; spawner.js keeps its event-driven exponent and gains onTeleport(rescaleCount?)/optional scaleMgr injection for the same resync.

Skytree base collider is disabled from EVT.GOAL_CONTACT until reset() so terrain never fights the finale MERGE ball-position writes (BOUNCE spam during the cinematic would shake the camera); spec's 'never released' refers to growth, and the run is over at contact.
EXTRA size-class pool partition frozen in curated.js: class0 = 70..79+81, class1 = 80/82/83/84/86 (decal goes mid), class2 = 85/87/88/89, class3 = 90/91/92.

Zone masks: 中央通り strip (x 4.6..18) is curated-only (T6 bit only) so the authored gutter/dressing street stays clean; bands 0/1 are allowed nowhere (the shop interior IS T0/T1, all curated); every zone carries bit 6 (T6 map-wide).

validateCityMap exempts スクランブル交差点 (landmarkId 5) from the strictly-increasing ladder assert — its spec 'absorbable @' entry is the T2-size crowd, not the r18 decal — and documents the 0.7m P3↔E-wall pocket (open dead-end, no seal possible on a flat floor; the 5 spec aisle pairs all assert >= 1.1m).
Curated reset() clears only curated-owned state (mirrors Spawner.reset's contract — resetWorld already reset store/pools/hashes before it runs).
deviations: TIER_UP re-banding is synchronous in the handler, not amortized in the 64/frame round-robin as the spec wording suggests — required for correctness (see keyDecisions); the amortized path remains as belt-and-suspenders.
validateCityMap's landmark-ladder strict monotonicity skips landmarkId 5 (スクランブル交差点) — the spec's own table marks its threshold as 'crowd T2-size', off the dioramaR ladder.

The spec text 'no pocket narrower than 1.1m exists' is not literally true of the frozen P3 (x1=3.9) vs E-wall (x=4.6) geometry: a 0.7m open pocket exists at z[-4.8,-3.6]. It cannot strand or seal (flat floor, open side); the validator prints it as documented instead of asserting on it.

Gutter-line 空き缶/ペットボトル/チラシたば/軍手 use existing T0/T1 archetype codes at the specced sizes (eraser/button_battery/game_soft/magazine_stack/toolbox/cardboard_box) per the '(T0/T1-scale recipes)' clause — no new archetype ids were invented (codes 0..69 are frozen).

spawner.onTeleport() needs a scale source: argless it only works if the integrator passes scaleMgr as the new optional 7th Spawner constructor arg (one-word main.js change); otherwise pass scaleMgr.rescaleCount explicitly. Without either it DEV-warns and keeps the stale exponent (today's Phase-0 behavior).
CityTerrain constructor takes an optional 3rd `scene` arg and exposes `terrain.mesh` — the frozen 2-arg signature works unchanged, but someone must add the mesh to the scene (see integration notes).

streams.approach: Read DESIGN-V3.md in full plus the Phase-0 contracts (types.js, events.js, tiers.js, tuning.js) and the current source of all four assigned files before editing. objects.js: added FLAG_CURATED=16, EXTRA_CODE_BASE=70, the frozen EXTRA_ARCHETYPE_IDS list (24 snake_case ids in code order 70..93 per the Phase-0 appendix), extended ARCHETYPE_ID_BY_CODE/ARCHETYPE_CODE_BY_ID to 94 entries, updated boot asserts (70 chunk / 94 total / no holes / no dup ids / reverse-map round-trip), and documented tierOf as curated-mutable. catalog.js: full Tokyo rewrite — 70 chunk archetypes matching the frozen tiers.js ids (v2 recipes reused where shapes match: eraser, paperclip, stool→丸イス, bookshelf→パーツ棚ラック, bicycle, person, sign→看板, cat, trash_can, utility_pole, torii, car, bus, truck, tree→街路樹, kiosk, house→町家, overpass→歩道橋, smokestack→銭湯の煙突, apartment→雑居ビル, gas_tank, crane, ferris_wheel, skyscraper, stadium, cruise_ship, hill→丘陵, v2 skytree recipe→code 93) plus ~45 new recipes, and 24 EXTRA curated archetypes (12 collectibles incl. dual-tag ハチ公像, 10 landmark singletons with FROZEN dioramaR/collisionScale from the landmark table, shop shell, Skytree display-name slot). Every entry carries displayNameJa (frozen Japanese strings) + naturalBand; exported DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE (string[94]), EXTRA_CATALOG (code-keyed), EXTRA_SIZE_CLASS_BY_CODE + EXTRA_POOL_CAPS (4 shared pool classes). ball.js: chunk table 60→70 with cross-assert against objects.js (70 chunk match + 94 total), knockOff skips rec.code >= EXTRA_CODE_BASE (EXTRA permanently stuck), slot-steal convention documented as load-bearing. absorb.js: constructor gains injected curated (4th param, already wired in main.js); stamps AbsorbEvent.archetypeCode and collectibleId (via curated.collectibleIdFor) next to the rare stamp, BEFORE store.free. Verified headless in node: all 94 geometries build, tri caps ≤350 held (total 14,345 tris), unit-bounding-sphere normalization exact, yOffsets machine-measured (-1 - minY of normalized geometry) and patched back into the file via codemod (41 entries). Executed the DEV-only boot-assert blocks by esbuild-bundling with import.meta.env.DEV=true — tiers/objects/catalog/ball asserts all pass, which resolves the documented Phase-0 shim ('npm run dev throws until Stream C lands').
keyDecisions: EXTRA id strings live in ONE place: objects.js EXTRA_ARCHETYPE_IDS (frozen code order); catalog.js imports it and dev-asserts entry-for-entry — no duplicated list to drift.

EXTRA archetypes are registered in CATALOG (94 keys) as well as EXTRA_CATALOG, so geometryFactory.buildAllGeometries(CATALOG) builds their geometries for the curated pools/thumbnails and absorb's collisionScale table covers codes 70..93 without new wiring.

Size-class pool assignment exported as EXTRA_SIZE_CLASS_BY_CODE + EXTRA_POOL_CAPS; assignment: collectible-small=70..79+81, landmark-mid=80/82/83/84/86(scramble decal rides mid), landmark-large=85/87/88/89, landmark-xl=90/91/92, code 93=null. Verified IDENTICAL to Stream B's already-landed curated.js EXTRA_POOL_CLASS Int8Array.

Frozen landmark numbers encoded as radiusNominal=dioramaR and collisionScale from the DESIGN-V3 table, with dev spot-asserts on the load-bearing five (ハチ公 1.2/cs1.0, 雷門 cs0.8, 東京駅 cs0.55, 橋スパン cs0.5, 東京タワー 170/cs0.45).

knockOff EXTRA skip implemented as rec.code >= EXTRA_CODE_BASE inside the ring walk (older chunk-coded entries are still found past stuck trophies); EXTRA codes fold to proxy families with the unchanged (code % ARCH_PER_TIER) & 7 formula.
yOffset values are machine-measured from the normalized geometries (same convention as v2) rather than estimated — only 'cloud' keeps an intentional non-rest value (+0.4, floats above ground).
EXTRA entries have spawnWeight 0 (dev-asserted) so they can never be chunk-rolled; chunk entries assert spawnWeight > 0 and naturalBand === tier.
T0 absorbable radii authored at 0.5-1.2cm (ネジ 0.006 etc.) so the 2cm start ball (absorb ≤ 0.65r = 1.3cm) has immediate targets, matching the shop interior cluster spec (objR 0.6-1.2cm).
deviations: T5 chunk landmarks sized 200 (丘陵) / 190 (湾岸コンビナート) instead of the v2-style 2.5-4x rule (would be 325-520): in v3 the next tier is truncated by the 420m goal, so 2.5-4x would make them never-absorbable; 200/190 are absorbable at ~307/292m (early-mid T6). Single-constant Phase-3 retune lever as designed.

丘陵 reuses the v2 'hill' grassy-dome recipe rather than the v2 'mountain' snow-capped recipe (the design's 'v2 mountain レシピ' note) — 丘陵 is a green hill and the snow-cap gradient read as winter; the frozen id 'mountain' and cs 0.85 are kept exactly. Trivial to swap back if the integrator prefers.

Code 93 (tokyo_skytree) carries a real buildGeometry (the v2 skytree recipe) although it is display-name-only: keeps buildAllGeometries uniform over CATALOG and costs ~120 tris at boot; it is never spawned (spawnWeight 0, sizeClass null — both dev-asserted).
The design freezes the export 'EXTRA_CATALOG' but not the size-class export names; I chose EXTRA_SIZE_CLASS_BY_CODE and EXTRA_POOL_CAPS (documented in the catalog header).

streams.approach: Read DESIGN-V3.md in full plus the Phase-0 contracts (types.js, events.js, tuning.js, index.html frozen ids) and the current source of all four assigned files before editing. collection.js (NEW) implements the frozen interface exactly: subscribes EVT.ABSORB, matches collectibleId>=0, ORs the frozen-id bit into a {v:1,mask} localStorage blob (shape-validated like RunStats._loadBest, unknown high bits preserved, append-only rule documented in the header), and emits EVT.COLLECT on the reused payload with nameJa from DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE[70+id]; prerenderThumbnails renders the 12 collectible archetypes into THUMB_SIZE_PX data-URLs via an offscreen WebGLRenderTarget + readRenderTargetPixels + 2D-canvas toDataURL (main canvas never touched, renderer state save/restore, try/catch, idempotent, headless-safe early-return that stays un-latched so the pre-approved lazy call-site move still works). runStats.js gains ScoreEvent.archetypeCode pass-through, an EVT.LANDMARK handler (+LANDMARK_SCORE_BONUS with one neutral EVT.SCORE emit so the HUD panel/float update), GoalEvent.collectFound from the injected collection (constructor arg + setCollection() late-binding for the frozen construction order), and all MOON_ consumers deleted (GOAL_SCORE_BONUS, EVT.GOAL_CONTACT). hud.js was rewritten for v3: absorb-name floats `+120 ネジ` with rolling per-code FLOAT_MERGE_S(0.30s) merging (`+150 ネジ x3`, textContent rewrite + animation restart, no new span), visible cap 3 mobile / 6 desktop from the 8-span pool with oldest-visible eviction and rare/EXTRA(code>=70) priority floats that never merge; timer m:ss below 768px and m:ss.t above (matchMedia 'change' re-renders live; display capped 99:59.9); dash ring writing BOTH --gauge (deg) and --gauge01 (0-1) on #dash-button at 10Hz with EVT.DASH snap and #dash-button.flash on DASH_READY; #absorbed-inline 'xN' writes; #collect-popup driver (thumbnail via injected collection, NEW marker, コレクション n/12, COLLECT_POPUP_S auto-out, toast max-width 58vw while visible); #goal-arrow rename with 64px-top/100px-bottom pixel exclusion clamps; landmark center toast; all EVT.MOON_* subscriptions deleted. screens.js adds the 4x3 result collection grid (frozen cell classes, 「？」 unfound, .cell-new badges, reveal at the 1.9s cue + skip-path + _clearReveal), #result-collect-n from GoalEvent.collectFound, the v3 X intent text on the untouched twitter.com/intent/tweet?text&url&hashtags mechanism, #donack-toggle wiring with LS_DONACK_KEY persistence and setDonack() hookup, and EVT.GOAL_CONTACT instead of the MOON alias. Verified with node --check plus an 83-assertion headless harness (/tmp/streamD-test/test.mjs, fake DOM/clock/localStorage): collection mask round-trip incl. high-bit preservation and malformed-blob rejection, runStats rank edges S240/A330/B450/C600 and bonuses, hud merge/cap/eviction/timer/gauge/popup/arrow behavior — all pass.
keyDecisions: DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE is consumed via a namespace import with [] fallback (import * as catalogModule) in hud.js/screens.js/collection.js so the live import graph keeps building before Stream C lands; after catalog.js ships the frozen string[94] the names appear with zero further changes (floats render plain '+N' until then).

RunStats supports BOTH constructor injection (4th arg, used by tests) and setCollection() late binding, because the frozen ABSORB order requires Collection to construct AFTER RunStats while main.js's current call passes the arg positionally.

EVT.LANDMARK in runStats emits one extra EVT.SCORE {delta: LANDMARK_SCORE_BONUS, combo: 1, rare: false, archetypeCode: -1} so the score panel and a plain '+8,000' float update immediately while sfx (rare-gated) and Donack (combo>=15-gated) see neutral values.

X post: the '#FableKatamari' line in the design's text template would double with the kept hashtags= param, so the tag rides ONLY hashtags= (mechanism untouched per the task brief); text is the v3 🗼/⏱/RANK/⭐/🏯レアn/12 template.

Result-grid NEW badge = first-EVER finds made this run (runMask only receives bits that were globally new), not re-collects of old album entries — a NEW badge on a previously-found cell would be a lie; CollectEvent.isNew already has the same all-runs semantics.
Collect-popup NEW indicator lives in the frozen #collect-popup-count line ('NEW!　コレクション n/12') — no unfrozen DOM ids invented.

Float merge window is rolling (anchored on the last same-code write) and each merge restarts the fly animation, which also re-extends visibility — matches the 'rewrites the live span and restarts its animation' spec verbatim.

Thumbnails render to an offscreen WebGLRenderTarget and readRenderTargetPixels into a scratch 2D canvas (flipped Y) -> toDataURL, so the visible title screen never flashes; clear color/alpha/render-target are saved and restored and the RT is disposed (exemptions ledger #5).
Goal-arrow exclusion zones are pixel clamps on the arrow CENTER: y in [64+28, h-100-28], x in [28, w-28] (56px arrow, center-anchored margins).
Timer cap 99:59(.9) applies to both formats; the breakpoint listener clears the diff cache and re-renders the timer so a rotation/resize flips format instantly.
deviations: Hud constructor gained an optional 2nd param `collection` (thumbnail source for #collect-popup) — not in the frozen changed-signatures list, default null so `new Hud(bus)` still boots; popup degrades to name+count without it.
Screens constructor gained an optional 3rd param `collection` plus a `setDonack(donack)` method (donack constructs after screens in boot order) — also not in the frozen list, both default-safe.
Collection exposes isFound(id)/isNewThisRun(id) beyond the frozen interface (consumed by screens.js for grid cells and NEW badges); frozen members are all present and exact.
RunStats.setCollection() added beyond the spec'd constructor injection (needed to satisfy the frozen construction order in main.js).
X intent text drops the literal trailing '#FableKatamari' from the design's template because the kept hashtags= param already supplies it (avoids a doubled hashtag).
DISPLAY_NAME_BY_CODE imported via namespace-with-fallback instead of a named import, so hud.js/screens.js (already in the live import graph) don't break `npm run build` before Stream C lands.

filesWritten: /Volumes/AIWorkSSD/AIWorkSpace/github/aieo-product/fableDemoGame/public/assets/donack/ (deleted 16 surplus frames; kept idle/happy/thinking/speaking -0/-3, 20,050 bytes total)
streams.approach: Read DESIGN-V3.md in full (including the Phase-0 implementation ledger appendix), the Phase-0 frozen contracts (events.js, tuning.js, types.js, index.html donack/collect markup), and the current source of every assigned file before editing. Implemented Stream E completely: (1) config/donackLines.js — the full frozen Japanese line table (43 lines keyed by frozen string ids, each {text, priority, expression, once, phase}) plus frozen lookup tables TIER_UP_LINE_IDS, LANDMARK_LINE_IDS, COLLECT_LINE_IDS, FIRST_LINE_BY_CODE (archetype codes from the frozen 70-id table) and DUAL_COLLECTIBLE_ID/DUAL_LANDMARK_ID. (2) ui/donack.js — Donack controller with the exact frozen interface (constructor(bus, initialOff), setOff(b)): 8-class webp avatar swap with frame-0/3 blink at 4 fps only while visible (the blink interval doubles as the auto-dismiss counter — no setTimeout), priority+min-gap cooldowns, queue-of-1 with equal/lower discard, per-id once-per-run dedupe + 30 s tip cooldown, phase gating {title,play,cinematic,result} with hard reset on GAME_RESET (1 Hz ticker demonstrably stopped), dual-tag merged-line case (COLLECT id 10 -> 'dual_hachiko', LANDMARK id 0 skipped), LS_DONACK_KEY persistence inside setOff, zero per-frame allocation (no rAF hook; prebound handlers; static strings). (3) scripts/verify-donack-assets.sh — POSIX sh gate asserting exactly the 8 kept webp frames, no strays, total <= 40 KB; verified green plus negative-tested (stray + missing frame both exit 1). Pruned the 16 surplus frames (24 -> 8 files, 20,050 bytes). (4) sfx.js — landmark fanfare sting on EVT.LANDMARK, collect 5-note gliss on EVT.COLLECT with microtask-deferred same-frame suppression when LANDMARK fires (dual-tag rule), goal-call pad rename, all MOON_ consumers deleted. (5) bgm.js — layer gates re-keyed to L1 t>=2 / L2 t>=3 / L3 t>=5, GOAL_CALL/GOAL_CONTACT renames, MOON_ consumers deleted. (6) backdrop.js — profile pair re-themed to 下町屋根並み (low roofline runs + sento-chimney spikes) <-> 富士山+湾岸スカイライン (bay tower skyline + one Mt. Fuji cone max-composited on the back layer), crossfade still keyed to T3, same geometry topology (1536 tris), determinism and the rescale pixel-identity law untouched. Storm-tested headless: scripts/storm-test-donack.mjs drives virtual time through the real Donack instance — 217 checks, 0 failures, covering phase gating, no-spam under a 120 s dense SCORE storm, queue priority, dual-tag, bubble-across-GOAL_CONTACT -> ascension -> result, GAME_RESET hard reset, tip cooldowns, setOff, and line-table shape.
keyDecisions: Line ids are frozen STRINGS (per the spec's 'line id -> {text,...}' shape); the dual-tag merged line is id 'dual_hachiko' — the design's nickname 'line #42'. The design's own table enumerates 43 lines despite the '~42' figure; all 43 are shipped and the storm test asserts the count.

sfx dual-tag suppression: COLLECT arrives BEFORE LANDMARK (frozen order), so the collect gliss is deferred ONE microtask via a prebound flush + one boolean (_collectPending); a same-frame LANDMARK clears it. Bounded (<= 12 collects/run) — documented against the allocation law.

sfx rare-sparkle gliss gated to ScoreEvent.archetypeCode < 70: curated collectibles are FLAG_RARE too, so without the gate every collect would double-gliss (rare + collect). Until Stream D stamps archetypeCode, v2 runStats leaves it -1 (< 70) so chunk rares keep sparkling through the transition — safe both sides.
Donack min-gap is measured from bubble DISMISSAL (not show) — the stricter anti-spam reading; the 120 s storm test confirms <= 1 bubble/20 s average.

Donack blink interval (4 Hz, visible-only) doubles as the show-duration counter — no setTimeout anywhere; the only other timer is the 1 Hz run ticker, started on GAME_START and cleared on GAME_RESET (asserted by the storm test, per the design's 'demonstrably stop' requirement).

ascension line is scheduled ASCENSION_LINE_DELAY_S = 3.5 s after GOAL_CONTACT by the 1 Hz ticker (contact shout ~3.5 s, ascension line lands mid-pullback, GAME_WIN's result line interrupts at ~8.7 s).
setOff(b) persists LS_DONACK_KEY itself ('1'/'0', try/catch) so whoever wires #donack-toggle gets persistence for free; idempotent if Stream D also writes the key.
Donack dual-tag handled statically (deterministic, no timing dependence): COLLECT_LINE_IDS[10] = 'dual_hachiko' and the LANDMARK handler skips landmarkId 0.
first-absorb categories mapped from the frozen archetype-code table: ネジ系 {0,1,2}, 生き物 {24,25}, 通行人 {21}, 車 {30-33}, ビル {40,41,50-53}.
repeat-bonk = 3 BOUNCE events with impactSpeed01 >= 0.5 inside a 60 s window (once per run); tip lines (tip_idle/tip_dash/tip_edge) are the only once:false lines, on the 30 s per-id cooldown.

backdrop SKYLINE_FROM_TIER stays 3 ('crossfade keyed to T3'); Fuji is max-composited over the bay skyline on the BACK layer only, wrap-safe, seeded bearing — geometry topology, tri count, draw count and the pure-function-of-(radius,camera) rescale law all unchanged.

Also scrubbed lowercase 'moon' comment mentions from sfx.js/bgm.js (events.js header demands src be moon-free outside environment.js's kept night cosmetic); bgm's legacy noise seed VALUE 0x6d6f6f6e kept (same noise texture), comment reworded.
deviations: Line count: the design says '42 lines' but its own authored table contains 43 entries; all 43 shipped verbatim. The merged ハチ公 line is referenced by frozen string id 'dual_hachiko' rather than a numeric '#42' (numeric position in the table is 40th — the '#42' label cannot be reproduced as an index without reordering the design's table).

Map-edge hint: the design says the 1 Hz idle/edge check is 'fed by GrowEvent', but GrowEvent carries NO position (types.js frozen shape). Added an OPTIONAL third constructor arg getBallPosReal() -> {x,z} (REAL meters); with it unwired (default null) the 'tip_edge' line simply never fires and everything else is self-contained. The frozen 2-arg interface is preserved (param is additive).
Donack GAME_START also clears per-run dedupe state (in addition to the GAME_RESET hard reset) so a retry flow that skips GAME_RESET still gets fresh once-per-run lines — strictly additive safety.

scripts/storm-test-donack.mjs is an extra file beyond the assigned list — it IS the stream-mandated headless storm test ('Storm-test Donack priority/cooldown headless'); pure dev tooling, imported by nothing in the build.

fixes: main.js full integration: real CityTerrain(bus, scaleMgr, renderer.scene) / CuratedSpawner / Collection / Donack / SkytreeView constructed at the V3-WIRE anchors in the frozen ABSORB order (spawner -> curated -> main attach -> runStats -> collection -> sfx/effects/hud); Spawner gained the 7th scaleMgr arg so the argless spawner.onTeleport() resyncs; curated.attachChunkSpawner(spawner) wired in DEV; effects.setCollectibleProvider wired; finale gets skytree as 3rd arg; runStats.setCollection / Hud(bus, collection) / Screens(bus, worldSeed, collection) / screens.setDonack(donack); collection.prerenderThumbnails kept at the title call site; resetWorld += terrain/curated/collection (+ real->sim bridge re-anchor); devTeleport switched to cityMap DEV_STARTS.

NEW src/render/extraPools.js (integration-owned): the spec's '4 SHARED InstancedPools' cannot render distinct geometry per instance (one InstancedMesh = one geometry), so the 4 EXTRA size-class pools are THREE.BatchedMesh-backed adapters (BatchedExtraPool) exposing the exact InstancedPool surface curated/ScaleManager consume (alloc/free/setTransform/setColor/fadeIn/fadeOut auto-free/update/flush/rescaleAll/rebaseAll/reset) — holds the ledger at exactly +4 draws with per-instance geometry chosen by EXTRA code. Caps 12/6/6/6 per Stream B's capacity audit. Registered in the instances Map under 'extra:N' keys so ScaleManager eachPool covers RESCALE/REBASE for free (spawner/curated only look pools up by archetype id), and in poolList for update/flush/reset.
curated.js 1-line contract extension: pool.alloc() -> pool.alloc(code) at the single activation alloc site (chunk InstancedPools ignore the arg; BatchedExtraPool selects geometry by it).

ABSORB attach handler hardened: POOL_BY_CODE snapshotted (slice) BEFORE EXTRA pools append (codes >= 70 must never resolve to chunk-pool indices); EXTRA branch reads tint via BatchedExtraPool.getColorHex and NEVER frees (EXTRA render slots are exclusively curated-owned, freed by deferred cleanup); store.instanceSlot[i] = -1 stamped after attachStuck (slot-steal convention, load-bearing per ball.js header — was missing from the v2 handler).

BUG FIX curated.forceScan(): mid-run devTeleport snaps worldScale with no RESCALE event, so still-in-ring active placements kept STALE sim coordinates in the store (reproduced: maneki-neko at sim (52.5,17.5) vs correct (10.5,3.5) after ?shop r=0.3 teleport — collidable at wrong position, collect test failed). forceScan now deactivates every active placement (consume=false, identity preserved by consumed bitmask) with defensive 3-band hash removal before the budget-free full pass re-materializes everything in fresh coordinates.

BUG FIX devTeleport: added terrain.reset() (the spec's 'terrain release re-eval') — terrain mesh scale/origin only synced on RESCALE/REBASE events, so a teleport left mesh.scale stale (observed 25 vs correct 5; walls rendered 5x too large); reset re-anchors to the snapped worldScale and re-arms the release latch for next-step re-evaluation.

MOON_ alias retirement: deleted EVT.MOON_CALL/GUIDE/CONTACT + the 3 PAYLOADS.moon* identity lines (events.js), the full tuning.js alias block incl. MOON_SCORE_BONUS and the descent/magnet relics; scrubbed comment-level MOON_ references in types.js/scaleManager.js/environment.js (MOON_PULSE_AMP renamed GLOW_PULSE_AMP). Gate: grep -rn 'MOON_' src == 0 except the sanctioned MOON_DIR_MIN_ELEV night-cosmetic exemption.

index.html Phase-0 shim deletion: #dash-gauge/#dash-gauge-fill (markup + 3 CSS blocks), #moon-arrow element + alias selectors, nested #absorbed-value flattened to x0; gauge-flash keyframes kept (still consumed by #dash-button.flash).
FLAG_CURATED unified: local const 16 in curated.js/spawner.js replaced by the objects.js export (value frozen, single source).

Donack map-edge hint wired: zero-alloc real->sim origin bridge in main.js (RESCALE *= S / REBASE += sx,sz subscriptions + reused {x,z} scratch) passed as Donack's 3rd getBallPosReal arg; reset in resetWorld and devTeleport.
DEV-only window.__v3dbg probe added next to devTeleport (stripped from prod) — used by the e2e harness; kept for future integration testing.
remainingIssues: Pacing runaway past the shop release (Phase-3, BINDING per design): one observed run cascaded ~4m -> 117m in ~3s holding W down the dense Akiba corridor (GROWTH_K=10 + curated street dressing chains); street-only growth at r=4 is normal (4.00->4.05 over 3 absorbs). The mandated >=3-playthrough Phase-3 retune (DENSITY_K_V3 / zone masks / rank thresholds) must address this — single-file data edits by design.

Real-device gate still owed (Stream D binding): one real iOS + one real Android pass (safe-area env(), @property --gauge ring interpolation, webp pixelated rendering). Devtools-matrix portrait 390x844 + desktop 1280x800 validated here only.

Toast/collect-popup can overlap a few px on 390px-wide portrait (58vw toast cap + 198px popup > 390px by construction); popup renders on top so it reads fine — cosmetic, Stream D band-matrix follow-up.

Stream A residual visual checks not screenshot-diffed: KeyR forced-rescale pixel-identity diff per tier and the env-silhouette -> goalTower mesh crossfade at ws 0.18-0.25 (silhouette never on-screen in the top-down shop shots). Functionally exercised (6 natural rescales to 441m, zero errors, finale contact exact) but the diff evidence pass remains for the QA/deploy phase.

BatchedMesh relies on WEBGL_multi_draw for the 1-draw guarantee (Chromium/Firefox/Safari 17+ OK); on browsers without it three.js falls back to looped draws — covered by the 72-cap headroom (worst observed 58) but worth one renderer.info glance on the real-device pass.

fps in the headless harness read 23-43 (shared-machine throttling); heap delta +0KB held everywhere. The iGPU/Android profile pass (governor, T4->T5 draw watch, Ueno<->Asakusa parked test) belongs to the deploy phase.

agent-browser desktop session accumulated two EMPTY '✗' error-buffer artifacts after an about:blank navigation hiccup; in-page error/unhandledrejection hooks captured zero real errors over live gameplay — harness artifact, not a page error.

?r=/?at= dev URLs re-apply their start radius on ROLL AGAIN (v2-inherited dev semantics): restart from a ?r=400 session re-enters the finale band at origin. Clean-URL restart verified exact (ws 0.04, r 0.02, finale idle, terrain re-armed, 238 curated re-streamed).
integration.approach: Integrated in the binding order B->C->D->E->A entirely through main.js plus minimal cross-file fixes. Read DESIGN-V3.md in full, mapped every stream's shipped API against the frozen interfaces, then resolved the one genuine architecture gap — the 4 shared EXTRA pools need per-instance geometry, impossible on InstancedMesh — with a BatchedMesh adapter (src/render/extraPools.js) that preserves the InstancedPool contract and the exact +4-draw ledger, requiring only a 1-arg alloc(code) extension in curated.js. Wired all constructions at the V3-WIRE anchors preserving the frozen ABSORB subscription order, finished devTeleport (cityMap DEV_STARTS + spawner.onTeleport via injected scaleMgr + curated.forceScan + terrain.reset), retired the MOON_ alias layer (grep gate green, MOON_DIR_MIN_ELEV sole exemption), and deleted the index.html transitional shims. Validation: npm run build green (752.19 kB / 204.29 kB gzip); npx vite dev boot with all stream DEV asserts + validateCityMap passing (411 placements, 94 geometries, aisle/growth-chain/ladder tables printed); agent-browser e2e at 390x844 (title -> start in shop -> joystick via synthetic TouchEvents -> absorb floats '+315 ネコ' -> dash ring --gauge/--gauge01 snap+refill -> Donack bubble in the mid-left band -> collect popup with data-URL thumbnail 'NEW! コレクション 1/12') and 1280x800 (keyboard WASD, shop release fade at 4m with mesh hide + no stuck states, all DEV_STARTS landing at exact real coords/ws/tier, finale e2e: GOAL_CALL -> #goal-arrow -> Skytree contact -> merge/ascension burst/night fade -> result with 12-cell grid (5 found) -> X intent URL decoded exact (twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=🗼FABLE KATAMARI…&url=fable-katamari.pages.dev&hashtags=FableKatamari) -> clean restart). Two mid-validation bugs found and fixed (stale curated coords + stale terrain transform on mid-run teleport), each re-verified in-browser. Overlay: 43-58/72 draw calls across shop/street/city, heap +0KB, zero console errors. Servers killed, sessions closed.
